topic: Tidal N2 (larger lunar elliptic semi-diurnal) period
our_argument_summary: N2 (12.66 hours) is a semi-diurnal lunar constituent with a UNIQUE physical origin: it exists specifically because the moon's orbit is elliptical (eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549). When the moon is closer (perigee), the M2 tidal amplitude increases; when farther (apogee), it decreases. This amplitude modulation at the lunar anomalistic month period (~27.55 days) creates the N2 beat frequency. The dome model places the moon at 2,534 km with no stated or derived orbital eccentricity. Without this parameter, N2 has no physical origin in dome geometry. Furthermore, N2 is the ONLY tidal constituent whose amplitude ratio to the principal tide directly encodes a fundamental orbital parameter (moon's eccentricity). The dome claims N2 as a confirmed WIN while conceding in OPEN-007 that 'the moon's orbital mechanics have no working dome-native explanation.' This is a textbook self-contradiction: claiming to predict a tidal signal whose very existence depends on orbital mechanics you have not solved. Verdict: Self-Contradicted.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: The tidal WINs (045-051) are V51.0 additions (April 2026). Confirmed via raw-text/08-live-power.txt: monitor.py handles M2, K1, and S2 tidal periods but NOT O1 or N2. N2 has no domain, no data fetch, no comparison logic. The claim is a static text assertion: 'N2 = 12.66 hours matches prediction.' No derivation from dome geometry is provided. No N2 amplitude calculation exists.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Not applicable — no monitoring code exists for this WIN. N2 is a static hardcoded period value in wins.json with no automated verification.
data_sources_used: N2 period = 12.66 hours (from Doodson 1921 harmonic tables and modern NOAA constituent database — a tabulated standard value, not a dome derivation)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Dome could argue: (1) 'Aetheric tides operate via undiscovered physics, including harmonic eccentricity mechanisms,' (2) 'Matching N2's period IS a prediction,' (3) 'Orbital mechanics are secondary to period matching.' All three are weak: (1) is unfalsifiable, (2) inverts the definition of prediction, and (3) contradicts the dome's own OPEN-007 which treats orbital mechanics as essential to lunar physics.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Our review correctly identifies that N2 has no monitoring infrastructure and the period is static.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Confirmed via raw-text/08-live-power.txt: monitor.py domain listing shows M2, K1, and S2 tidal domains but N2 is absent. No pull_data.py routine fetches N2 constituent data. No automated comparison logic. N2 was added to wins.json in V51.0 as a static claim with no computational pipeline.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: N2 has been catalogued since Doodson (1921), derived from the tide-generating potential and lunar orbital mechanics. Standard tidal theory explains N2 as the amplitude modulation of M2 caused by the moon's eccentricity: N2/M2 ≈ 3e (for small e). The dome model adopts the known period 12.66 hours and relabels the mechanism as 'aetheric' without deriving the eccentricity or changing any numerical prediction. The period is pure relabeling of standard physics.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: N2's period has been known and tabulated for over a century (Doodson 1921 derived the complete harmonic expansion; N2 was recognized in the 1800s in tidal analysis). The dome model claimed it as a WIN in V51.0 (April 2026). The 'prediction' is a century-old known value declared confirmed retroactively.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No dome-specific derivation connects N2 to dome geometry. N2 arises from the beat between M2 (lunar semi-diurnal, period 12.42 hours) and Mf (lunar fortnightly, period ~13.66 days): 1/N2 = 1/M2 - 1/Mf. This requires the moon to have an elliptical orbit with eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549 — a parameter the dome model has never specified, derived from dome geometry, or incorporated into any calculation. The dome's own OPEN-007 states 'the moon's orbital mechanics have no working dome-native explanation.' Without orbital mechanics, N2 is undefined in the dome framework.

holes_found.description: Cycle 1 recommended restructuring detail_evidence to LEAD with the eccentricity argument, since N2 is uniquely dependent on it. The current text buries the eccentricity point in the middle: 'N2 specifically arises from the moon's elliptical orbit (eccentricity e ≈ 0.055); without a published eccentricity parameter...' This is correct content but wrong emphasis. For N2 specifically (unlike M2/S2 which just need a distant body, or K1/O1 which need orbital motion), the crux is that the amplitude ratio N2/M2 directly encodes the moon's eccentricity. A dome defender cannot hand-wave this: if the dome claims N2, it must derive e ≈ 0.0549. The Cycle 1 review correctly identified this as the strongest N2-specific argument, and it should be the OPENING, not a buried clause.
holes_found.recommendation: Restructure detail_evidence to lead with: 'N2 (12.66 hours) is uniquely distinguished among the five tidal WINs because its very existence requires the moon to have an elliptical orbit with specific eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549. The amplitude ratio N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 directly encodes this eccentricity (approximately N2/M2 ≈ 3e for small e). The dome model has never specified an orbital eccentricity for its local moon at 2,534 km altitude, nor derived one from dome geometry. Without this parameter, N2 has no physical origin in dome mechanics — it is the tidal constituent most directly dependent on orbital mechanics, the very mechanics the dome's OPEN-007 concedes are unresolved.' Then follow with the spatial pattern argument (one spike vs two bulges).

holes_found.description: The finding text remains generic: 'Period is trivial (lunar elliptical); dome's local moon cannot produce the observed two-bulge tidal pattern.' This is technically correct for N2 (it IS semi-diurnal, so two-bulge language applies) but it's identical to the findings for WIN-045 and WIN-046. The finding does not capture what makes N2 unique: the eccentricity requirement. WIN-050 (O1, diurnal) correctly has differentiated language from WIN-049 (K1, diurnal) about diurnal inequality vs bulge count. WIN-051 should have equally differentiated language from WIN-045/046 emphasizing the eccentricity issue.
holes_found.recommendation: Revise finding to: 'Period is trivial (amplitude encodes lunar eccentricity); dome's local moon has no derived orbital eccentricity — N2's physical origin is absent from dome geometry.' This directly signals the eccentricity requirement and differentiates N2 from the other semi-diurnal constituents.

holes_found.description: The detail_claim remains terse: 'Tidal N2 period matches dome model.' For the most eccentricity-dependent tidal constituent, this undersells the specificity. Compare WIN-045's equally terse claim with WIN-050's fuller context language. For N2, the claim should hint at what makes it different: its dependence on orbital parameters.
holes_found.recommendation: Expand to: 'The N2 (larger lunar elliptic semi-diurnal) tidal constituent period of 12.66 hours matches the dome model, but N2's physical origin (lunar orbital eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549) is not specified in dome geometry.' Or more concisely: 'The N2 (lunar-eccentricity-modulated semi-diurnal) tidal constituent period of 12.66 hours matches the dome model prediction.' The key point is to signal in the detail_claim itself that N2 is orbit-dependent.

holes_found.description: The detail_verdict_text is extremely terse: 'Period is trivial. Dome's local moon produces a sharp localized spike, not the observed global two-bulge pattern.' The Cycle 1 review correctly noted this is a 2-layer argument (period is trivial; pattern is wrong) but the verdict text only touches layer 2. For N2, the verdict should emphasize layer 3: 'You also need to explain the eccentricity.'
holes_found.recommendation: Expand verdict text to: 'Period is trivial. Dome's local moon produces a sharp localized spike, not the observed global two-bulge pattern. Most critically, N2's amplitude ratio N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 encodes the moon's orbital eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549, a parameter the dome model has never derived from its geometry — making N2 the eccentricity-dependent test the dome cannot pass.'

holes_found.description: The detail_evidence references OPEN-007 ('See...Doodson (1921)') but does not explicitly cite the dome's OPEN-007 concession. The Cycle 1 review noted in kernel_of_truth: 'The dome's OPEN-007 explicitly concedes that the moon's orbital mechanics have no working dome-native explanation — the very mechanics that generate N2.' This is a powerful self-contradiction that should be cited directly in detail_evidence.
holes_found.recommendation: Add sentence to detail_evidence: 'The dome's own OPEN-007 acknowledges that lunar orbital mechanics—the mechanism generating N2 through orbital eccentricity modulation—have no working dome-native explanation, contradicting any claim that N2 is a confirmed dome prediction.'

stronger_arguments.description: N2/M2 amplitude ratio as an eccentricity test: The observed ratio N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 in real tides directly constrains the moon's orbital eccentricity. Standard tidal theory predicts N2/M2 ≈ 3e for small eccentricities, yielding e ≈ 0.063 (close to actual e ≈ 0.0549). If the dome model claims to predict N2, it must also predict N2's amplitude relative to M2. If the dome cannot derive the ratio, it has not predicted N2 — it has only recalled the period. This is a quantitative falsification test: show the dome's calculation of N2/M2 from its geometry. No such calculation exists.
sources: Doodson, A.T. (1921). 'The Harmonic Development of the Tide-Generating Potential.' Proc. Royal Society A, 100, 305-329. doi:10.1098/rspa.1921.0088
Pugh, D.T. & Woodworth, P.L. (2014). Sea-Level Science: Understanding Tides, Surges, Tsunamis and Mean Sea-Level Changes. Cambridge University Press, §3.2 (harmonic constituent relationships)

stronger_arguments.description: N2 and the lunar nodal cycle: N2 exhibits a secondary 18.61-year modulation due to the regression of the lunar orbital nodes (nodal argument). This modulation is a direct consequence of lunar orbital mechanics and is precisely predicted by celestial mechanics. If the dome claims N2, it must explain this 18.61-year cycle without reference to the globe's lunar orbital dynamics. The dome has provided no nodal-cycle derivation from its local moon geometry.
sources: Foreman, M.G.G. (1977). Manual of Harmonic Analysis and Prediction of Tides. Department of Fisheries and Oceans, Ottawa.

stronger_arguments.description: The spring-neap cycle signature: N2 beats with M2 to produce the 14.7-day spring-neap cycle (maximum tidal range near full/new moon, minimum at quarter moons). This is observable at every tide gauge worldwide and is one of the most robust tidal patterns. The pattern is driven by the constructive/destructive interference between M2 (main lunar tide) and its amplitude-modulated variant N2 (caused by lunar eccentricity). If the dome's local moon at 2,534 km has no specified eccentricity, it has no mechanism to produce the amplitude modulation that creates the N2 frequency, and therefore cannot produce the observed spring-neap cycle through standard tidal mechanisms. A dome defender must either (a) derive the moon's eccentricity from dome geometry, or (b) invoke 'aetheric' mechanisms to generate the spring-neap cycle independently — but invoking unfalsifiable 'aetheric' mechanisms is exactly the kind of evasion this review exists to expose.
sources: NOAA Tide Predictions (tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov) — global tide gauge database with routine spring-neap modulation at all stations
Cartwright, D.E. (1999). Tides: A Scientific History. Cambridge University Press, §6 (harmonic analysis and constituent relationships)

kernel_of_truth.description: N2 is a real and important tidal constituent — the modulation of the principal lunar semi-diurnal tide (M2) caused by the moon's elliptical orbit. It has typical open-ocean amplitudes of 2–6 cm and is catalogued in every modern tidal constituent database. The dome model correctly identifies its period (12.66 hours) and recognizes that a complete tidal theory must account for orbital perturbations beyond the basic M2/S2 pair. Including N2 demonstrates awareness that tidal dynamics involves multiple orbital timescales (the orbital eccentricity, the lunar nodal cycle, etc.) beyond a simple two-body-at-fixed-distance model.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Stating a period catalogued in 1921 is taxonomy, not prediction. N2 is uniquely demanding because its existence REQUIRES the moon to possess an elliptical orbit with eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549. The amplitude ratio N2/M2 directly encodes this eccentricity: changing e changes the N2/M2 ratio proportionally. The dome model has never: (1) specified the moon's eccentricity, (2) derived it from dome geometry, or (3) calculated N2's amplitude or amplitude ratio. Furthermore, the dome's OPEN-007 explicitly concedes that 'the moon's orbital mechanics have no working dome-native explanation.' This is a direct self-contradiction: claiming N2 as a confirmed prediction while acknowledging that the orbital mechanics generating N2 are unresolved. Matching a known period without deriving the amplitude, the spatial pattern, or the underlying orbital parameters is equivalent to copying the answer from a textbook without showing the work.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 2 assessment: The Self-Contradicted verdict is absolutely solid. This is one of the strongest cases in the entire review, stronger even than the other tidal WINs. Here's why:

1. N2 is ECCENTRICITY-DEPENDENT: Unlike M2 (which just requires a moon to exist), S2 (which requires the sun), or even K1/O1 (which require orbital motion), N2 specifically requires the moon to have a non-zero elliptical orbit. This is not a secondary detail — it's the physical origin of the constituent.

2. The N2/M2 ratio is a quantitative test: The ratio ≈ 0.19 directly constrains e ≈ 0.0549. If the dome cannot derive this ratio from its geometry, it has failed to predict N2 in any meaningful sense. A dome defender cannot hand-wave this away by saying 'aetheric mechanisms are different' — the ratio is observable data that pins down the eccentricity, and no aetheric mechanism the dome has proposed can explain why the N2/M2 ratio is exactly the value predicted by standard orbital mechanics.

3. The self-contradiction with OPEN-007 is explicit: The dome's own unresolved problem list states that lunar orbital mechanics are not explained in dome geometry. N2 exists because of lunar orbital mechanics (specifically eccentricity). Therefore, the dome cannot predict N2 without resolving OPEN-007. Claiming N2 while OPEN-007 is unresolved is claiming a prediction for a physical phenomenon you've admitted you can't explain.

4. Cycle 1 correctly identified this: The Cycle 1 review found that N2 is 'the most geometrically demanding of all five tidal WINs' and recommended leading with the eccentricity argument. This recommendation was not implemented in wins.json — the current text buries it and leads with the generic two-bulge argument instead.

Recommended action is MAJOR_REWRITE because:
- The detail_evidence needs structural reorganization (lead with eccentricity, not bury it)
- The finding needs to be differentiated (not generic two-bulge language)
- The detail_claim should hint at the eccentricity dependence
- The detail_verdict should emphasize the N2/M2 amplitude ratio test
- A direct citation of OPEN-007 should be added

However, the verdict itself (Self-Contradicted) does not need to change. The confidence increases slightly from 0.94 (Cycle 1) to 0.96 (Cycle 2) because the discovery of N2's unique eccentricity requirement makes the self-contradiction even clearer than the generic two-bulge argument.

The comparison with WIN-050 (O1) is instructive: WIN-050's Cycle 1 review found the same structural issue (generic two-bulge language that's technically correct but misleading for a diurnal constituent), and WIN-050 is now flagged in Cycle 2 for needing the same restructuring. The pattern suggests the tidal WINs have not yet been consistently refined to emphasize their unique physical signatures: M2 (principal term), S2 (solar forcing), K1/O1 (declination effects), N2 (eccentricity effects). Current language treats all five as generic 'two-bulge vs. one-spike' comparisons, missing the nuance that makes each one distinct and harder to hand-wave.

N2 is the apex of this pattern: it's the only tidal constituent whose amplitude ratio directly measures an orbital parameter. That makes it the most powerful test case and the most important one to rewrite clearly.

citations_verified: Doodson (1921) DOI 10.1098/rspa.1921.0088 — 'The Harmonic Development of the Tide-Generating Potential' in Proc. Royal Society A, Vol. 100, No. 704, pp. 305-329. Verified across all tidal WIN reviews (045, 049, 050). This paper derived the complete harmonic expansion of the tide-generating potential and identified hundreds of harmonic constituents including N2.
N2 period 12.66 hours — Standard tabulated value from Doodson (1921) and all modern tidal constituent databases (NOAA, IHO). High confidence.
N2/M2 amplitude ratio ≈ 0.19 — Standard textbook value; relationship N2/M2 ≈ 3e is first-order approximation from tidal theory. Verified in Pugh & Woodworth (2014), Chapter 3.
Lunar eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549 — Astronomical constant, verified in JPL ephemeris and celestial mechanics references.
citations_unchecked: 18.61-year nodal cycle — standard astronomical value, not independently verified in this review but extremely well-established in celestial mechanics
Spring-neap cycle 14.7 days — standard value derived from M2 (12.42h) and S2 (12.00h) beat frequency; mathematical derivation straightforward but not re-derived in this review
